channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Port Fairy pool to be demolished due to 'financial madness' of repair bill
item.description: The pool's shuttering has been met by community uproar, with some locals rallying to protest the closure and calling for its reopening.
item.dc:creator: Jean Bell

media:group.media:description: Belfast Aquatics in Port Fairy has been closed after mould and algae were found in the facility.

item.title: Breaking: Kumanjayi White's family told no charges to be laid over his death
item.description: The family of the 24-year-old man who died while in police custody at an Alice Springs supermarket last year has released a statement after hearing no charges would be laid over his death.
item.dc:creator: Elsie Lange

media:group.media:description: Kumanjayi White's family have released a statement after hearing no charges will be laid over his death.

item.title: Breaking: Two men arrested by Dezi Freeman taskforce
item.description: Detectives investigating the movements of police killer Dezi Freeman have arrested two men in Victoria's north-east.

media:group.media:description: Dezi Freeman was on the run for seven months before he was shot by police in March.

item.title: Why Trump's push to expand his Abraham Accords was met with silence
item.description: As the US president pushes for a deal with Iran, he is reviving one of the signature foreign policy projects of his first term: the Abraham Accords. Here's what to know about the series of agreements, and whether Arab countries will answer his call to formally establish diplomatic ties with Israel.
item.dc:creator: Audrey Courty

media:group.media:description: Benjamin Netanyahu, Donald Trump and Abdullah bin Zayed display their copies of signed agreements at a ceremony for the Abraham Accords in 2020.

item.title: Late pastoralist's family loses appeal over judgement that father was responsible for bushfire
item.description: A court judgement that found pastoralist Tom Brinkworth liable for a 2021 bushfire, five months after he died, has been upheld after an appeal by Mr Brinkworth's family.
item.dc:creator: Eugene Boisvert

media:group.media:description: Smoke rises near Lucindale as a fire threatens the town.

item.title: Baz Luhrmann partners with Gold Coast on proposed creative arts precinct
item.description: The council wants to repurpose an existing depot into a major creative arts precinct that it says will generate $130 million annually and create 1,000 jobs.
item.dc:creator: Heloise Vyas

media:group.media:description: An artist's impression of the planned Miami Arts Depot on the southern Gold Coast.

item.title: Jazz legend Sonny Rollins dies at 95
item.description: Rollins, one of the greatest saxophonists of all time, played with everyone from Miles Davis to The Rolling Stones.

media:group.media:description: Sonny Rollins performed from the 1940s until ill health forced his retirement in 2014.

item.title: Can State of Origin save an NRL season that's losing its way?
item.description: Players, coaches and fans are disillusioned with the rule changes that have come to define this NRL season but ahead of Origin I can the interstate series put a broken season back together?
item.dc:creator: Nick Campton

media:group.media:description: The NRL season needs a strong Origin series like few times before.

item.title: How a broken leg set off a 13-hour helicopter rescue in rough seas
item.description: The pilot says the remote retrieval of the fisherman was the most challenging rescue of his career.
item.dc:creator: Ellie Willcox and Jeremy Jones

media:group.media:description: Emergency services completed a 13-hour offshore rescue after a fisherman broke his leg on a trawler off the central Queensland coast.

item.title: Youth escapee avoids recording conviction for knifepoint carjacking of tourists
item.description: One of the teenagers involved in a group escape from the Ashley Youth Detention Centre in December is sentenced for his involvement in the armed robbery of two tourists.
item.dc:creator: Monty Jacka

media:group.media:description: The teen carjacked two tourists in the north-west Tasmanian town of Penguin.

item.title: I'm making a conscious effort to break free from unspoken family trauma
item.description: Motherhood has a way of making family patterns impossible to ignore. Suddenly you start asking questions you never asked before.
item.dc:creator: Carlie Schofield

media:group.media:description: Carlie Schofield pictured as a baby, with her mum.

item.title: How to check if your vaccines are up to date as diphtheria outbreak continues
item.description: Do you know your vaccination history? Some vaccines need to be updated regularly, especially if you are travelling. Here's what you need to know to ensure you stay protected.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Roberts

media:group.media:description: If you're unsure about your vaccination history, the best thing to do is chat to your GP.

item.title: Defence industry could rival WA resources sector under weapons hub plan
item.description: Missiles for use in the Australian military could soon be made in Western Australia with the government seeking to establish a local weapons manufacturing hub, the third state to do so.
item.dc:creator: Gian De Poloni, Kate Christian, Neve Brissenden, and Gabrielle Becerra Mellet

media:group.media:description: Roger Cook has announced details of the plan as WA seeks a bigger role in Australia's defence sector.

item.title: Notorious children's jail could become minimum-security adult prison farm
item.description: The Tasmanian government says Ashley Youth Detention Centre could become a minimum-security adult prison and "agriculture-based prison rehab and training facility".
item.dc:creator: Lucy MacDonald

media:group.media:description: Ashley Youth Detention Centre in Deloraine is expected to close in 2028.

item.title: Serious crash involving road train closes Stuart Highway in far north SA
item.description: A major outback highway in South Australia's north is closed in both directions after a crash involving a road train and a vehicle early this morning.
item.dc:creator: Jenae Madden

media:group.media:description: The Stuart Highway is Australia's primary north-south transcontinental corridor.

item.title: Man's death confirmed as diphtheria-related after overseas toxicology test
item.description: The Northern Territory government says the rate of new diphtheria cases in the territory has fallen from more than 20 per week to less than 10 after 10,000 Territorians stepped up to be immunised against the ongoing outbreak.
item.dc:creator: Jason Walls

media:group.media:description: Steve Edgington says the government's diphtheria response has been "swift" and "ongoing".

item.title: 170m chimneys come crashing down as power station demolition begins
item.description: The demolition of the Liddell Power Station, which powered homes and businesses in NSW for more than 50 years, is underway.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Bernasconi and Courtney Yeandle

media:group.media:description: The moment the chimneys at the Liddell power station were demolished.

item.title: ACL injury appears to end Warriors legend's NRL career
item.description: Warriors club legend Roger Tuivasa-Sheck appears to have played his last NRL game after confirming he suffered an anterior cruciate ligament injury in round 12.

media:group.media:description: Roger Tuivasa-Sheck will miss the rest of the NRL season.

item.title: SA Police return to Yunta as part of Gus Lamont investigation
item.description: Police have returned to Yunta in South Australia's far north as part of their investigation into the disappearance of four-year-old boy Gus Lamont.
item.dc:creator: Sara Garcia

media:group.media:description: Gus Lamont went missing from his family's property in September last year.

item.title: SA Police investigating theft of organ pipes from heritage-listed former church
item.description: The pipe theft comes days after a bronze statue was stolen from the centre of a fountain in Adelaide's inner north-eastern suburbs.

media:group.media:description: Police say damage was done to a heritage-listed organ during an alleged break-in.

item.title: Scathing warning to people harassing royal commission witnesses
item.description: Four officers were present at the event when the shooting started, with Jewish security organisation Community Security Group NSW denied its request to have police on patrol for the entirety of the event.
item.dc:creator: Phoebe Pin

media:group.media:description: Virginia Bell says the royal commission is keeping a close eye on any instances of intimidation or harassment of witnesses.

item.title: US military strikes Iranian boats and missile launch sites
item.description: US Centcom says the strikes were designed "to protect our troops from threats posed by Iranian forces".

media:group.media:description: The US military says the strikes are a defensive action.

item.title: Two arrested in Vietnam over Sydney gang boss shooting
item.description: Authorities in Vietnam have arrested two men after Sydney crime figure Lorenzo Lemalu was shot dead in Ho Chi Minh City last week.

media:group.media:description: Lorenzo Lemalu, who was shot dead in Vietnam last week.

item.title: Tasmania 'just wanted to roll trucks in' as quick fix for icebreaker refuelling saga
item.description: A federal department says the Tasmanian government's proposed interim solution to a costly refuelling saga affecting Australia's Antarctic icebreaker is "far from" acceptable.
item.dc:creator: Jano Gibson

media:group.media:description: The RSV Nuyina docked at Hobart on October 16, 2021.

item.title: Government sets up fight with Santos over gas reservation plan
item.description: The draft framework outlines a requirement for gas producers to supply 20 per cent of their export to the domestic market, which the gas lobby argues will "undermine" the industry.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Mercer

media:group.media:description: Madeleine King says the reservation will put downward pressure on domestic gas prices.
